chief_complaint: A 32-year-old woman presents with fever and palpitations.
history: The patient has a history of Graves' disease on methimazole therapy and was diagnosed with COVID-19 infection a week ago.
exam: She was febrile and tachycardic with fine tremors on examination.
labs: Serum thyroid-stimulating hormone levels are <0.01 microIU/ml, with elevated T4 and T3, and agranulocytosis. Burch-Wartofsky Point Scale score was 60 points.
